candidates.headline: Probate online to go live for solicitors nationwide on the Courts Portal from 2 June 2026
candidates.factual_basis: The Courts Service said solicitors nationwide could apply for grants of probate online through the Courts Portal from 2 June after a Dublin pilot processed more than 100 applications and prompted solicitor-feedback updates.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A direct Irish court-modernisation story affecting probate filing, solicitor workflows, Revenue integration, turnaround expectations, and the wider Courts Service modernisation programme.

candidates.headline: Law Society educators help develop European AI training tools for lawyers
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that Law Society education staff joined the European Commission-funded LITEL 2 project to develop AI-related training for lawyers across Europe.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A high-priority Irish legal-education item showing lawyer training being redesigned around AI skills, GDPR, IP, core legal judgment, agentic AI, and EU-wide training toolkits.

candidates.headline: Law Society opens free Social Media, AI and the Law MOOC with more than 3,000 registrations
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that registration opened for the Law Society's free on-demand Social Media, AI and the Law course, beginning on 9 June with more than 3,000 participants signed up.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows strong Irish demand for public and professional legal education on AI, social-media liability, privacy, cybercrime, ecommerce, digital manipulation, and responsible online practice.

candidates.headline: Irish co-founded legal AI startup Wordsmith raises USD 70 million Series B
candidates.factual_basis: Irish Legal News reported that Wordsmith AI secured a USD 70 million Series B, is used by more than 500 in-house legal teams, and plans go-to-market hiring and expansion in Ireland.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A strong Irish-linked legal AI funding story focused on in-house legal intake, workflow routing, outside-counsel reduction, activity measurement, and Irish customer and talent expansion.

candidates.headline: Private firm certified to adjudicate DSA platform-user disputes from Ireland
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that Coimisiun na Mean certified Impress Dispute Resolutions as an out-of-court dispute-settlement body under the Digital Services Act for the five years from 14 May 2026 to 14 May 2031.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Important for digital-rights and platform-governance lawyers because it creates an online dispute route for platform decisions involving illegal content, complaint handling, independence, and procedural accessibility.

candidates.headline: ICCL raises concerns over reported nationwide garda bodycam rollout
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported ICCL concern about reports of a EUR 150 million nationwide garda body-worn camera rollout before publication of the pilot assessment report or a finalised code of practice.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to criminal defence, policing oversight, surveillance governance, data protection, public consultation standards, and digital-evidence workflows in Irish criminal justice.

candidates.headline: EU Justice Scoreboard shows Ireland high on trust but low on some justice-system digital indicators
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that the 2026 EU Justice Scoreboard ranked Ireland in the top half for public online justice information but fourth from bottom for procedural rules allowing digital technology in court.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Gives Irish readers comparative evidence on court digital readiness, ADR incentives, justice-system efficiency, judge resources, and where modernisation pressure may fall next.

candidates.headline: Universities told to preserve critical thinking as AI adoption accelerates
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that RDJ's Universities Conference heard universities must move beyond AI adoption to lead responsible use, preserve critical thinking, and adapt procedures for student issues and investigations.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A useful Irish education and legal-risk item linking AI literacy, graduate pathways, fair procedures, investigations, and governance design in institutions served by education and public-law lawyers.

candidates.headline: Law Society says District Court criminal legal-aid flat-fee plan is open to court challenge
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that the Law Society described the proposed EUR 455 flat fee for District Court criminal legal-aid cases as seriously flawed and open to legal challenge ahead of a planned July start.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A major Irish service-delivery and access-to-justice item because fee design may affect criminal-defence availability, case-handling incentives, vulnerable defendants, and the viability of District Court representation.

candidates.headline: Survey sends clear message on flat-fee plan for District Court criminal legal aid
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported a Law Society survey in which 70% of criminal-law solicitors said they would be unlikely or very unlikely to continue District Court criminal legal-aid work under the proposed flat-fee model.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Useful supporting evidence on the same Irish criminal legal-aid issue, showing how payment design could change practitioner participation and delay access to representation.

candidates.headline: International Protection Act 2026 takes effect with new asylum timelines and appeals body
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported that the International Protection Act 2026 came into effect on 12 June, introducing screening, mandatory two-to-six-month timelines, and the new Tribunal for Asylum and Returns Appeals.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A major legal-service-delivery change for immigration and public-law practitioners because it redesigns asylum processing, appeals infrastructure, timelines, border procedures, and rights-monitoring roles.

candidates.headline: Irish presidency discussion highlights fully digital EU Inc company-law model
candidates.factual_basis: The Gazette reported an ICEL webinar on the proposed 28th Regime and EU Inc model, including online company formation within 48 hours, digital shares and registers, online meetings, and Irish presidency expectations for negotiations.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to legal-service design, company formation, cross-border advisory work, digital registers, venture financing, and how corporate-law workflows may become more standardised and online across the EU.

candidates.headline: UK government plans AI legal assistants for Crown Court backlog pilot
candidates.factual_basis: The Guardian reported that David Lammy would announce AI-powered virtual legal assistants for Crown courts, with judges planning tools to identify trial-ready cases and group similar hearings.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A major UK justice-innovation story for courts, criminal practitioners, legal-aid policy, AI governance, backlog management, and comparison with Irish court digitisation efforts.

candidates.headline: UK Government launches AI Growth Lab with legal as first focus area
candidates.factual_basis: Legal IT Insider reported that the UK Government's advisory AI Growth Lab will first focus on LawTech, legal services, and conveyancing, with the ICO working with the CLC, SRA, and Legal Services Board on cross-regulatory AI challenges.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Important for legal AI builders and adopters because regulators are trying to clarify how existing rules apply to legal-service AI while supporting responsible adoption and access to justice.

candidates.headline: European Commission publishes code of practice for marking and labelling AI-generated content
candidates.factual_basis: The Commission published a voluntary code of practice setting practical steps for providers and deployers of generative AI systems to meet AI Act transparency obligations applying from 2 August 2026.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to lawyers advising AI providers, deployers, publishers, regulated clients, legal publishers, and law firms using GenAI in public-facing material or client workflows.

candidates.headline: AI Act enforcement gets independent expert support
candidates.factual_basis: The European Commission appointed a Scientific Panel and an Advisory Forum to support AI Act enforcement, including GPAI model classification, systemic risks, evaluation methods, standardisation, and implementation challenges.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: EU AI governance architecture is becoming operational, shaping legal advice on AI suppliers, legal AI tools, compliance evidence, auditability, and AI-risk controls for regulated clients.

candidates.headline: DeepIP acquires PatentMaker to target European leadership in patent AI
candidates.factual_basis: LawSites reported that AI patent drafting platform DeepIP acquired PatentMaker, a German patent workflow tool, and would open a German office inside Boehmert & Boehmert's Munich premises.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Signals consolidation in European IP AI tooling and possible workflow change across invention capture, prior-art search, drafting, filing, prosecution, and post-grant patent work.

candidates.headline: Former PwC partner and ex-SYKE COO launch AI legal services company Telon
candidates.factual_basis: Legal IT Insider reported that Lewis Bretts and Tom Mellor launched Telon with a 15-person team, delivery across four countries, a major platform partnership, and an Outcomes Engine for managed AI legal work.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A new legal-service-delivery model combining AI platforms, lawyers, agents, playbooks, managed services, and outcome pricing rather than traditional software-only or hourly-services models.

candidates.headline: Clio acquires Jurisage to support Canadian legal AI products
candidates.factual_basis: Legal IT Insider reported that Clio acquired Jurisage, bringing structured Canadian legal data including Compass, which covers more than 470,000 cases across 43 courts and is updated daily.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows jurisdiction-specific legal data becoming strategic infrastructure for legal AI, with implications for matter strategy, drafting, research, and small-firm platform competition outside the US.

candidates.headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 launch draws legal-sector attention for agent benchmarks and governance concerns
candidates.factual_basis: Legal IT Insider reported that Claude Fable 5 was made available through legal AI platforms including Harvey, achieved top scores on Harvey's Legal Agent Benchmark and BigLaw Bench, and raised legal governance issues over privacy and data retention.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Material for firms and legal departments because frontier-model capability, legal-agent performance, benchmark selection, security controls, and client-data governance all shape adoption decisions.

candidates.headline: Claude for Legal has more than 90 workflow agents
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Claude for Legal includes more than 90 named end-to-end workflow agents, with source-attribution, jurisdiction-setting, and explicit gates before work is filed, sent, or relied on.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A substantive legal AI product-development item because it shifts attention from general assistants to configurable legal workflows for contracts, DSARs, litigation, clinics, and law-school use.

candidates.headline: Sandstone raises USD 30 million for AI-native in-house legal teams
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported Sandstone's USD 30 million Series A led by Lightspeed Venture Partners for a platform connecting counterparties, stakeholders, matters, obligations, contracts, and history for in-house teams.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows investor backing for legal relationship management, intake, triage, drafting, review, knowledge retrieval, and AI-native infrastructure for general counsel and legal operations.

candidates.headline: Kirkland and Palantir partner for private equity platform
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Kirkland & Ellis and Palantir launched a proprietary enterprise platform for private equity fundraising as part of a multiyear partnership using Palantir's AI Platform.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A high-end legal-service delivery story showing a major firm productising senior-lawyer judgment, institutional knowledge, and complex fund-formation workflows across more than 1,000 investment-funds lawyers.

candidates.headline: Legora buys Cadastral agentic real estate platform
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Legora acquired Cadastral, a 10-person AI agent platform for commercial real estate, and planned to establish a New York engineering hub.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Shows legal AI platforms expanding into adjacent transaction-heavy verticals where due diligence, leases, underwriting, zoning, refinancing, disputes, and document review drive legal workflows.

candidates.headline: LawGeex founders launch Superlegal NewMod firm
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that LawGeex co-founder Noory Bechor and Ilan Admon launched a regulated AI-first Superlegal law firm in Utah focused on construction contracting for SMBs, with attorney sign-off on reviews.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to alternative business structures, AI-enabled legal-service delivery, attorney-in-the-loop contracting, fixed-fee review, and competition between legal tech platforms and traditional firms.

candidates.headline: Ironclad founder Jason Boehmig joins OpenAI for legal vertical launch
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Ironclad founder Jason Boehmig joined OpenAI to lead product for the legal vertical and said OpenAI plans a legally focused range of tools.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Material for law firms, in-house teams, and legal tech vendors because it suggests another frontier AI provider is moving directly into legal workflow products and legal-market distribution.

candidates.headline: AmLaw 200 firm Hanson Bridgett goes all-in with Claude
candidates.factual_basis: Artificial Lawyer reported that Hanson Bridgett adopted Claude firm-wide for attorneys and professional staff, using it across document review, drafting, research, organisation, internal operations, due diligence, and knowledge management.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A concrete law-firm AI adoption example involving general models, legal add-ons, enterprise data protections, written AI policy, workflow review, and change management for professional staff as well as lawyers.

candidates.headline: Smokeball free trust-accounting program reaches more than 900,000 US lawyers
candidates.factual_basis: LawSites reported that Smokeball partnered with 28 state bar associations to offer free trust accounting and billing software to members, reaching more than 900,000 lawyers after adding Florida and North Carolina.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: Relevant to small-firm operations, compliance, access to modern practice tools, bar association technology partnerships, and closing gaps in trust-accounting adoption.

candidates.headline: Mississippi federal judge sanctions four lawyers over AI-hallucinated court filings
candidates.factual_basis: Business Insider reported that US District Judge Sharion Aycock removed four lawyers from a case, barred two from appearing before the court for two years, and imposed USD 8,000 in fines after AI-generated bogus citations appeared in filings.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A practical AI-governance warning for lawyers and firms because it reinforces verification duties, local-counsel risk, litigation workflow controls, and sanctions exposure from unreviewed AI output.

candidates.headline: Eve launches EveOS as an AI-native operational platform for plaintiff firms
candidates.factual_basis: LawSites reported that Eve launched EveOS, an AI-native operating system for plaintiff firms, adding four products including a live case data layer, operational analytics, communication agents, and embedded legal research, with more than 1,400 plaintiff firms and 200,000 active matters on the platform.
candidates.legal_sector_relevance_note: A meaningful legal-workflow story because it moves beyond point AI drafting into firm-level operations, case lifecycle data, attorney review gates, AI agents, intake, research, and case-management integration.
